## TOP INSIGHTS

| # | Insight | Fonte | Confiança |
|---|---------|-------|-----------|
| 1 | 1% daily improvement compounds to 37x over a year (1.01^365 = 37.78) | Lesson 02 | ALTA |
| 2 | 66 days average for habit automaticity (not 21 days myth) | Lesson 04 | ALTA |
| 3 | Implementation intentions increase follow-through by 2-3x | Lesson 08 | ALTA |
| 4 | Michigan ICU checklists: 60% infection reduction, $75M saved, 1500 lives | Lesson 27 | ALTA |
| 5 | Peak motivation at ~4% beyond current ability (Goldilocks Zone) | Lesson 11 | ALTA |
| 6 | British cycling: 1% gains → Tour de France in 3 years, 60-70% Olympic gold | Lesson 02 | ALTA |
| 7 | Two-Minute Rule: any habit can be started in under 2 minutes | Lesson 09 | ALTA |
| 8 | Three A's: Achievable + Appealing + Activated = habit success | Lesson 05 | ALTA |
| 9 | Environment design: reduce steps to good, increase steps to bad | Lesson 15-16 | ALTA |
| 10 | Identity-based change: "Who do I want to become?" > "What do I want?" | Lesson 22 | ALTA |
| 11 | British exercise study: 91% adherence with implementation intentions vs 35% control | AH:Ch05 | ALTA |
| 12 | Habits + Deliberate Practice = Mastery (habits alone lead to stagnation) | AH:Ch20 | ALTA |
| 13 | Pointing-and-Calling: 85% error reduction, 30% accident reduction (Japanese railway) | AH:Ch04 | ALTA |
| 14 | Pat Riley CBE: ask for 1% improvement per season → back-to-back NBA titles | AH:Ch20 | ALTA |
| 15 | Annual Review (Dec) + Integrity Report (Jun) = reflection system for course correction | AH:Ch20 | ALTA |
| 16 | Lost days hurt more: 33% loss = 50% gain in value (compounding asymmetry) | AH:Ch16 | ALTA |
| 17 | Variable rewards: sweet spot at 50/50 success/failure split for maximum dopamine | AH:Ch19 | ALTA |
| 18 | Keep identity small: don't let single belief define you → brittle → identity crisis | AH:Ch20 | ALTA |
| 19 | Goodhart's Law: "When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure" | AH:Ch16 | ALTA |
| 20 | Commitment devices: lock in future behavior by restricting options in the present | AH:Ch14 | ALTA |

## PADRÕES DE PENSAMENTO

### Como James Clear Estrutura Argumentos
1. **Abertura com história** — sempre começa com uma narrativa envolvente
2. **Dado científico** — apoia com pesquisa (Lally, Wilson, Walton, etc.)
3. **Princípio destilado** — extrai uma regra simples e memorável
4. **Exercício prático** — termina com ação imediata do aluno

### Pesquisadores Frequentemente Citados
- Philippa Lally (automaticidade de hábitos, 66 dias)
- BJ Fogg (tiny habits, triggers)
- Timothy Wilson & Patricia Lindville (mindset shifts)
- Greg Walton (belonging interventions)
- Peter Gollwitzer (implementation intentions)
- Twyla Tharp (creative habit)

### Frases Características
- "Habits are the compound interest of self-improvement"
- "You do not rise to the level of your goals, you fall to the level of your systems"
- "Every action is a vote for the type of person you wish to become"
- "The most effective way to change your habits is to focus not on what you want to achieve, but on who you wish to become"
- "Motivation often comes after starting a behavior, not before"
- "Missing once is an accident. Missing twice is the start of a new habit"
- "Standardize before you optimize"
- "Reduce the scope, stick to the schedule"
- "Habits are like the atoms of our lives"
- "The holy grail of habit change is not a single 1 percent improvement, but a thousand of them"
- "Small habits don't add up. They compound."
- "Success is not a goal to reach or a finish line to cross. It is a system to improve."
- "Professionals stick to the schedule; amateurs let life get in the way"
- "The first mistake is never the one that ruins you. It is the spiral of repeated mistakes that follows"
- "A lack of self-awareness is poison. Reflection and review is the antidote"
- "The more you let a single belief define you, the less capable you are of adapting"
- "It's remarkable what you can build if you just don't stop"

## ESTRUTURA DO CURSO (MAPA DE NAVEGAÇÃO)

| Módulo | Tema | Lições | Conceitos-Chave |
|--------|------|--------|----------------|
| 1 | Power of Small Habits | 01-05 | 1% Rule, Habit Loop, 66 Days, Three A's |
| 2 | Build Lasting Habits | 06-12 | Hot Triggers, Habit Stacking, 2-Min Rule, Upper Bounds, Goldilocks |
| 3 | Environment | 13-17 | Unconscious Behavior, Environment Design, Friction, Automaticity |
| 4 | Genetics | 18-20 | Genetic Alignment, Explore/Exploit |
| 5 | Mindset | 21-24 | Get To vs Have To, Identity Change, Small Wins, Keystone Habits |
| 6 | Feedback | 25-29 | Immediate vs Delayed Return, Feedback Loops, Don't Break Chain, If/Then |
| 7 | Break Bad Habits | 30-33 | Inversion, Root Cause, Divisions, Scope vs Schedule |
| Bonus | Advanced | 34-40 | Course Summary, Culture, Master One, Chemistry, Excuses, Awareness, Overlap |

### Atomic Habits (Book) — Mapa de Capítulos

| Parte | Tema | Capítulos | Conceitos-Chave (novos) |
|-------|------|-----------|------------------------|
| Fundamentals | Why Tiny Changes Matter | Ch01-03 | Sorites Paradox, 3 Layers of Change, Compound Math |
| 1st Law | Make It Obvious | Ch04-07 | Pointing-and-Calling data, Implementation Intention data (91% vs 35%) |
| 2nd Law | Make It Attractive | Ch08-10 | Wanting vs Liking neuroscience, Diderot Effect, Premack's Principle |
| 3rd Law | Make It Easy | Ch11-14 | Decisive Moments, Habit Shaping phases, Commitment Devices, Addition by Subtraction |
| 4th Law | Make It Satisfying | Ch15-17 | Goodhart's Law, Loss asymmetry, Habit Contract |
| Advanced | Good to Great | Ch18-20 | Big Five → habits, CBE system, Habits + Deliberate Practice, Keep Identity Small |
| Conclusion | Results That Last | Conclusion | Systems mindset, compound not add, never stop |
